agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being scoped to allow customers visibility into their delivery's progress. Two internal stakeholder teams — Customer Success and Operations — have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements regarding whether a courier's real-time GPS location should be shown to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Shipment Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person physically transporting a package from origin to customer destination."},{"term":"Real-time Location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery","definition":"The end-to-end process from dispatch of a package to handoff at the customer's address."},{"term":"Tracking","definition":"The customer-facing capability to monitor the progress or status of their delivery."},{"term":"Active Delivery Window","definition":"The time period from courier dispatch to delivery confirmation, during which tracking is relevant."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A delivery must have a trackable status visible to the customer from dispatch through completion.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without any tracking status, the customer has no awareness of delivery progress, which is a fundamental expectation of any courier system.","testable_assertion":"Given an active delivery, the customer can always retrieve a current status (e.g., dispatched, in transit, nearby, delivered)."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Courier location data must be collected during active deliveries for operational purposes (routing, ETAs, dispatch optimization).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"GPS telemetry is essential for the logistics platform to function — ETA calculation, route optimization, and proof of delivery all depend on it.","testable_assertion":"Given a courier on an active delivery, the system records GPS coordinates at defined intervals regardless of customer-facing visibility settings."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"The level of location detail exposed to customers is a business policy decision that must balance customer transparency with courier privacy and driver retention.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is the core policy conflict. The granularity of what customers see (exact GPS vs. approximate zone vs. ETA-only) is a business decision, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"The system supports configurable visibility levels for customer-facing courier location data, and the active policy is set by authorized business stakeholders."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Courier personally-identifiable location data must not be exposed in a way that creates safety or privacy risks for the courier outside the delivery context.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Exposing a courier's exact, persistent location creates real physical safety risks and potential legal liability. This is a hard constraint regardless of business policy.","testable_assertion":"Given a completed delivery, the customer can no longer see any courier location data. Given an active delivery, any location data shown is scoped to delivery context only."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has an active delivery in transit","when":"They open the tracking view","then":"They see a delivery status and an estimated time of arrival (ETA) at minimum."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A delivery has been completed or cancelled","when":"The customer views the order","then":"No courier location or position data is displayed; only final delivery status is shown."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A courier is on an active delivery","when":"The system collects GPS telemetry","then":"The data is stored for operational use regardless of customer-facing visibility policy."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"The business has configured a location visibility policy","when":"A customer views tracking","then":"The system displays location information at the configured granularity (exact, approximate zone, ETA-only, or none) and no finer."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A courier is within a defined proximity of the delivery address","when":"The customer checks tracking","then":"The customer receives a 'courier nearby' notification or status update, providing actionable transparency without persistent surveillance."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier app loses GPS signal or courier goes offline mid-delivery","expected_behavior":"Customer sees last known status with a stale-data indicator (e.g., 'Last updated 5 min ago') rather than a frozen pin that implies the courier is stationary."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or records courier location data during delivery","expected_behavior":"System cannot prevent screenshots, but exact GPS should not be exposed in the first place under a privacy-respecting policy. Approximate zones mitigate this risk."},{"scenario":"Multiple deliveries assigned to the same courier","expected_behavior":"Each customer sees tracking only for their own delivery. No customer can infer the courier's route for other deliveries."},{"scenario":"Courier is on a break or paused between stops","expected_behavior":"Customer tracking shows 'delivery in progress' status only — no indication of courier being stationary at a non-delivery location."},{"scenario":"Customer contacts support asking for exact courier location","expected_behavior":"Support agents may have access to precise location for operational troubleshooting, but the policy for customer-facing disclosure must match the configured visibility level."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Getting this wrong in either direction has direct revenue impact. Losing couriers (Operations' concern) increases recruitment and training costs — typically 3-5x more expensive than retention. Losing customers due to poor tracking (Customer Success' concern) reduces order volume. The optimal solution must minimize both risks.","operational":"Courier retention is an existential operational concern — without drivers, there is no service. A tracking feature that causes driver attrition degrades delivery capacity and increases delivery times for all customers.","user":"Customers expect delivery visibility — this is table stakes in 2026 logistics. However, 'visibility' does not necessarily mean 'exact GPS coordinates.' ETA, status updates, and proximity alerts often satisfy the transparency need without surveillance.","risk":"Privacy and safety risk to couriers if exact location is exposed. Potential regulatory risk under GDPR/CCPA if courier location data is shared with third parties (customers) without proper consent framework. Reputational risk if drivers publicly complain about surveillance. Competitive risk if tracking experience is worse than alternatives.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Desire real-time delivery visibility to plan their availability. Impact depends on what granularity of tracking is offered — ETA and proximity alerts may satisfy most use cases."},{"group":"Couriers / Drivers","impact":"Directly affected by surveillance concerns. Exact GPS sharing creates feeling of being watched, contributing to attrition. Their consent and comfort level is critical."},{"group":"Customer Success Team","impact":"Needs a tracking feature they can market as a differentiator. Must be educated that 'transparency' can be achieved without exact GPS."},{"group":"Operations Team","impact":"Owns driver retention metrics. Needs assurance that any tracking feature will not exacerbate driver churn."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Must review any courier location sharing for privacy regulation compliance (GDPR, CCPA, local labor laws)."},{"group":"Product Leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate between the two teams and set the visibility policy. This decision cannot be made by engineering."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"Two explicit, non-negotiable stakeholder requirements directly contradict each other. The spec cannot be implemented as stated by both teams simultaneously. Until product leadership arbitrates the visibility policy, the exact customer-facing behavior cannot be defined. Confidence is low because the core feature definition is unresolved.","high_confidence_areas":["GPS telemetry must be collected regardless of customer visibility — this is an operational invariant","Tracking data must be scoped to active deliveries only — no post-delivery courier exposure","The system architecture should support configurable visibility levels to decouple the policy decision from implementation","ETA and status-based tracking are non-controversial and can proceed"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact granularity of customer-facing location data — this is the unresolved conflict","Whether couriers must opt-in or consent to any location sharing with customers","Regulatory requirements around sharing employee location data with third parties"],"assumptions":["The platform already collects courier GPS telemetry for operational purposes","Product leadership has the authority to arbitrate between Customer Success and Operations","A middle-ground solution (approximate location, ETA-based tracking) is technically feasible"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires exact GPS coordinates be shown to customers at all times during delivery, calling it non-negotiable. Operations requires courier location never be shown to customers, also non-negotiable, citing measurable driver attrition. These two requirements are mutually exclusive as stated and cannot both be satisfied.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Operations","Couriers","Customers","Product Leadership"],"options":["Accept Customer Success position: show exact GPS — risks continued driver attrition","Accept Operations position: show no location at all — risks losing competitive differentiation","Compromise: show approximate location zones (e.g., '3 blocks away') or ETA-only with proximity alerts — provides transparency without surveillance","Tiered approach: let couriers opt into location sharing granularity, with ETA as the guaranteed minimum"],"recommendation":"Escalate to Product Leadership for arbitration. Recommend Option C (approximate zones + ETA + proximity alerts) as the domain-correct middle ground. This is the industry standard approach used by major logistics platforms — it satisfies the customer's actual need (knowing when to be available) without creating the surveillance dynamic that drives courier attrition. Engineering should build a configurable visibility system so the policy can be adjusted without code changes. Do NOT proceed with implementation until this conflict is resolved at the executive level."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Cannot implement 'exact GPS coordinates at all times' as specified by Customer Success","business_rule_broken":"BR-4 — courier safety and privacy invariant. Exact persistent GPS exposure creates safety risks and likely violates privacy regulations for employee location data shared with third parties."},{"violation":"Cannot implement 'never show location' as specified by Operations without business validation","business_rule_broken":"BR-3 — the visibility level is a policy decision that requires executive arbitration, not a unilateral operations veto. Some level of delivery visibility is a market expectation (BR-1)."}]}
